question: Take the gender split from the 2011 Bulgarian census about those who have completed tertiary education. Subtract the smaller number from the larger number, then return the difference in thousands of women. So if there were 30.1 thousand more men, you'd give "30.1"

research_queries: 2011 Bulgarian census tertiary education gender statistics
Bulgaria 2011 census higher education men women
Bulgarian National Statistical Institute 2011 census tertiary education
Bulgaria 2011 population census university education by sex
2011 Bulgaria census completed tertiary education male female
working_plan: Search for 2011 Bulgarian census data on tertiary education by gender
Find the specific numbers for men and women who completed tertiary education
Use code to subtract smaller from larger number
Convert result to thousands format as requested
Verify the calculation and format matches example

solver.reasoning_summary: Found the official 2011 Bulgarian census data from the National Statistical Institute showing women with tertiary education: 791.8 thousand (22.3%) and men with tertiary education: 556.9 thousand (16.7%). Calculated the difference: 791.8 - 556.9 = 234.9 thousand more women than men with tertiary education. Verified calculation using code execution.

tool_calls.name: sandbox_exec

arguments.code:
# 2011 Bulgarian census tertiary education data
women_tertiary = 791.8  # thousand women with tertiary education
men_tertiary = 556.9    # thousand men with tertiary education

# Calculate difference
difference = women_tertiary - men_tertiary

print(f"Women with tertiary education: {women_tertiary} thousand")
print(f"Men with tertiary education: {men_tertiary} thousand") 
print(f"Difference: {difference} thousand")
print(f"Since women > men, the answer is: {difference}")

stdout=Women with tertiary education: 791.8 thousand

1. Find the report put out by the Bulgarian on the 2011 census by searching.
2. Find the requested data under the Educational Structure Section of the Report.
